lessons.title: Hello & Welcome
grammar: Verb "to be" (am/is/are) — positive forms
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-001"
pronunciation: [{"point":"The English Alphabet","explain":"English has 26 letters but many more sounds. Start with the names of the letters.","alphabet":[{"l":"A","ipa":"/eɪ/"},{"l":"B","ipa":"/biː/"},{"l":"C","ipa":"/siː/"},{"l":"D","ipa":"/diː/"},{"l":"E","ipa":"/iː/"},{"l":"F","ipa":"/ef/"},{"l":"G","ipa":"/dʒiː/"},{"l":"H","ipa":"/eɪtʃ/"},{"l":"I","ipa":"/aɪ/"},{"l":"J","ipa":"/dʒeɪ/"},{"l":"K","ipa":"/keɪ/"},{"l":"L","ipa":"/el/"},{"l":"M","ipa":"/em/"},{"l":"N","ipa":"/en/"},{"l":"O","ipa":"/əʊ/"},{"l":"P","ipa":"/piː/"},{"l":"Q","ipa":"/kjuː/"},{"l":"R","ipa":"/ɑː/"},{"l":"S","ipa":"/es/"},{"l":"T","ipa":"/tiː/"},{"l":"U","ipa":"/juː/"},{"l":"V","ipa":"/viː/"},{"l":"W","ipa":"/ˈdʌb.əl.juː/"},{"l":"X","ipa":"/eks/"},{"l":"Y","ipa":"/waɪ/"},{"l":"Z","ipa":"/zed/"}],"extension":"Did you know? The letters A, E, I, O, U are vowels. All other letters are consonants. In English, one letter can often represent multiple different sounds!","visual":"🔤"}]
cando: "Can greet people and say goodbye"
hw: "Learn the alphabet song"

lessons.title: What is your name?
grammar: Verb "to be" — questions (What is...?)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-002"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Contractions","explain":"In spoken English, we often combine words. 'What is' becomes 'What's'.","examples":[{"pattern":"What is","ipa":"/wɒt ɪz/","word":"What's"},{"pattern":"I am","ipa":"/aɪ æm/","word":"I'm"},{"pattern":"My name is","ipa":"/maɪ neɪm ɪz/","word":"My name's"}],"extension":"Contractions make your English sound more natural and fluent. We use them almost all the time when speaking!","visual":"🔗"}]
cando: "Can introduce themselves"
hw: "Practice spelling your name"

lessons.title: Numbers 1-20
grammar: Singular and Plural nouns (intro)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-003"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Numbers -teen vs -ty","explain":"Stress is key to distinguish numbers like 13 and 30.","examples":[{"pattern":"13","ipa":"/ˌθɜːˈtiːn/","word":"thirteen"},{"pattern":"30","ipa":"/ˈθɜː.ti/","word":"thirty"}]}]
cando: "Can count from 1 to 20"
hw: "Count objects in your room"

lessons.title: Minimal Pairs 1: Vowels
grammar: Basic sentence structure
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-004"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Short vs Long Vowels","explain":"Short sounds vs long sounds can change the meaning of a word.","minimalPairs":[{"w1":"ship","p1":"/ʃɪp/","w2":"sheep","p2":"/ʃiːp/"},{"w1":"bit","p1":"/bɪt/","w2":"beat","p2":"/biːt/"},{"w1":"fit","p1":"/fɪt/","w2":"feet","p2":"/fiːt/"}],"extension":"The length of the vowel is very important in English. Notice how your mouth stays in a similar position but the sound lasts longer for 'sheep' and 'beat'.","visual":"🚢↔️🐑"}]
cando: "Can distinguish between short and long vowel sounds"
hw: "Record yourself saying ship and sheep"

lessons.title: Essential Phrases
grammar: Polite requests (Please, Thank you)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-005"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Word Stress","explain":"The most important part of the word is louder and longer.","examples":[{"pattern":"Please","ipa":"/pliːz/","word":"please"},{"pattern":"Thank you","ipa":"/ˈθæŋk.juː/","word":"thank you"},{"pattern":"Excuse me","ipa":"/ɪkˈskjuːz.miː/","word":"excuse me"}]}]
cando: "Can use basic polite phrases"
hw: "Use three polite phrases tomorrow"

lessons.title: A day in my life — routines revisited
grammar: Present simple: all persons + frequency adverbs (mid-position)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-01"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Starter Foundation: The Alphabet Sounds","explain":"Master the vowel sounds in the alphabet which often cause confusion.","examples":[{"pattern":"A, H, J, K","ipa":"/eɪ/","word":"Alphabet"},{"pattern":"E, B, C, D, G, P, T, V","ipa":"/iː/","word":"Email"},{"pattern":"I, Y","ipa":"/aɪ/","word":"Identity"}],"tip":"English vowels 'A' and 'E' are often swapped by learners from other languages!"},{"point":"The Schwa Sound /ə/","explain":"The most common sound in English. It's short, weak, and found in unstressed syllables.","examples":[{"pattern":"a","ipa":"/ə'baʊt/","word":"about"},{"pattern":"er","ipa":"/'tiːtʃə/","word":"teacher"},{"pattern":"o","ipa":"/'pəʊlɪs/","word":"police"}],"tip":"Relax your mouth completely to make this sound."}]
speaking: "Compare routines with 2 partners using frequency adverbs"
listening: "Extended daily routine (3 speakers)"
reading: "A \"day in the life\" magazine article"
writing: "Full daily routine paragraph (8 sentences + frequency)"
task: "Class routine survey: who does what, how often?"
cando: "Can describe daily routines using all frequency adverbs"
hw: "Write a detailed weekly routine with frequency adverbs"

lessons.title: My job — describing work in detail
grammar: Present simple: "I work with/for/in; I am responsible for…"
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-02"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Word Stress in Occupations","explain":"Multi-syllable words always have one syllable that is stronger than the others.","examples":[{"pattern":"oo","ipa":"/ˌen.dʒɪˈnɪər/","word":"engineer"},{"pattern":"o","ipa":"/ˈfəʊ.tə.ɡrɑː.fər/","word":"photographer"},{"pattern":"a","ipa":"/ˈmæn.ɪ.dʒər/","word":"manager"}],"tip":"Tap your finger on the desk for the stressed syllable."}]
speaking: "Describe your job in 5 sentences to a partner"
listening: "3 job descriptions from real professionals"
reading: "A short job description from a job advert"
writing: "Job description for your own role (6 sentences)"
task: "Job description swap: guess the job from description"
cando: "Can describe what their job involves using present simple"
hw: "Write a job description as if for a LinkedIn profile"

lessons.title: My commute and workplace
grammar: "How long does it take? / How far is it?" + It takes / It is
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-03"
pronunciation: [{"point":"The /θ/ and /ð/ Sounds","explain":"English 'th' has two sounds: unvoiced /θ/ (think) and voiced /ð/ (the).","examples":[{"pattern":"th","ipa":"/θɪŋk/","word":"think"},{"pattern":"th","ipa":"/ðə/","word":"the"},{"pattern":"th","ipa":"/'ɜː.θɪŋ/","word":"earth"}],"tip":"Put your tongue between your teeth and blow."}]
speaking: "Tell a partner about your commute in detail"
listening: "Two people discussing their commutes"
reading: "Short article on commuting in cities"
writing: "Paragraph about your commute and workplace"
task: "Commute debate: which is the best way to travel?"
cando: "Can discuss commute and workplace location"
hw: "Write a 60-word commute description"

lessons.title: Earning and spending — money vocabulary
grammar: How much do you earn/spend? Present simple + object; too + adjective / enough
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-04"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Numbers and Word Stress","explain":"Differentiate between -teen and -ty numbers using stress.","examples":[{"pattern":"teen","ipa":"/ˌθɜːˈtiːn/","word":"thirteen"},{"pattern":"ty","ipa":"/ˈθɜː.ti/","word":"thirty"},{"pattern":"teen","ipa":"/ˌfɔːˈtiːn/","word":"fourteen"}],"tip":"The '-teen' is long and high; the '-ty' is short and low."}]
speaking: "Discuss spending habits: what do you spend most on?"
listening: "People discussing financial habits"
reading: "Short article on managing money"
writing: "Monthly budget plan in full sentences"
task: "Budget challenge: live on €1,500/month (group task)"
cando: "Can discuss earning and spending habits"
hw: "Write a paragraph about your financial habits"

lessons.title: My home — renting and buying
grammar: Have to / don't have to (obligation intro); How much does it cost?
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-05"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Vowel Sound /eɪ/ (Long A)","explain":"Common in words like 'name' and 'station'. It's a diphthong (two sounds in one).","examples":[{"pattern":"a_e","ipa":"/neɪm/","word":"name"},{"pattern":"ai","ipa":"/peɪ/","word":"pay"},{"pattern":"ay","ipa":"/deɪ/","word":"day"}],"tip":"Open your mouth then move your jaw up slightly."}]
speaking: "Role-play: tenant phones landlord about a flat"
listening: "A rental enquiry phone call"
reading: "A property advert"
writing: "Email to a landlord asking about a flat"
task: "Flat hunting: compare two properties and decide"
cando: "Can ask about and describe a rental property"
hw: "Write a short email enquiry about a flat to rent"

lessons.title: Food and health — what you eat, how you feel
grammar: Should / shouldn't for advice: "You should eat more… / You shouldn't…"
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-06"
pronunciation: [{"point":"The /i:/ and /ɪ/ Sounds","explain":"Differentiate between long 'ee' (feel) and short 'i' (ill).","examples":[{"pattern":"ee","ipa":"/fiːl/","word":"feel"},{"pattern":"i","ipa":"/ɪl/","word":"ill"},{"pattern":"ea","ipa":"/iːt/","word":"eat"}],"tip":"Think of a 'smile' for the long /i:/ sound."}]
speaking: "Give a partner 3 pieces of health advice"
listening: "A nutritionist giving advice"
reading: "A healthy eating guide"
writing: "5 health advice sentences using should/shouldn't"
task: "Health clinic: one person describes habits, partner gives advice"
cando: "Can give and receive basic health advice using should"
hw: "Write a 5-tip healthy lifestyle guide"

lessons.title: Technology in my life
grammar: Present simple + adverbs of frequency + like + -ing for tech habits
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-07"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Silent 'e' in Tech Words","explain":"The final 'e' is often silent but changes the previous vowel sound.","examples":[{"pattern":"_one","ipa":"/fəʊn/","word":"phone"},{"pattern":"_ine","ipa":"/'ɒn.laɪn/","word":"online"},{"pattern":"een","ipa":"/skriːn/","word":"screen"}],"tip":"The vowel before the consonant becomes 'long' because of the magic 'e'."}]
speaking: "Discuss your technology habits with a partner"
listening: "People describing tech habits"
reading: "A short survey on technology use"
writing: "A paragraph about your technology use"
task: "Tech debate: is your phone your best friend?"
cando: "Can describe technology habits using present simple"
hw: "Write: \"How I use technology every day\""

lessons.title: My interests and free time
grammar: Love / like / enjoy / don't mind / hate / can't stand + -ing (gradable scale)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-08"
pronunciation: [{"point":"The /-ing/ Ending /ŋ/","explain":"The 'g' is not fully pronounced. It's a nasal sound made at the back of the throat.","examples":[{"pattern":"ing","ipa":"/ˈpleɪ.ɪŋ/","word":"playing"},{"pattern":"ing","ipa":"/ˈrɪː.dɪŋ/","word":"reading"},{"pattern":"ing","ipa":"/ˈkʊk.ɪŋ/","word":"cooking"}],"tip":"Keep the back of your tongue up against the roof of your mouth."}]
speaking: "Discuss free time preferences using the full scale"
listening: "A leisure preferences survey"
reading: "A \"My free time\" blog entry"
writing: "\"My interests\" paragraph (6–8 sentences)"
task: "Interest match: find someone with the same top hobby"
cando: "Can express interests with gradable opinion verbs"
hw: "Write a short paragraph about your ideal weekend"

lessons.title: My neighbourhood and community
grammar: There is/are + quantity; adjectives for opinions; comparatives review
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-09"
pronunciation: [{"point":"The /v/ and /b/ Sounds","explain":"Many learners confuse these. /v/ is a 'fricative' (vibration), /b/ is a 'plosive' (stop).","examples":[{"pattern":"v","ipa":"/'vɪl.ɪdʒ/","word":"village"},{"pattern":"v","ipa":"/'veɹ.i/","word":"very"},{"pattern":"b","ipa":"/'bɪl.dɪŋ/","word":"building"}],"tip":"For /v/, your top teeth should touch your bottom lip."}]
speaking: "Describe your neighbourhood to a partner"
listening: "Two people comparing neighbourhoods"
reading: "A \"Where I live\" blog post"
writing: "Neighbourhood description (6 sentences)"
task: "My ideal neighbourhood: design and present"
cando: "Can describe and evaluate a neighbourhood"
hw: "Write a \"Where I live\" paragraph"

lessons.title: Unit 1 Review — My life today
grammar: Present simple (all forms) + should + have to + frequency + like -ing
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-10"
speaking: "Oral: describe your life today (3 minutes, all topics)"
listening: "Extended listening — 3 speakers describing their lives"
reading: "Full \"My adult life\" profile"
writing: "\"My life today\" paragraph (150 words)"
task: "Speed networking: 2 min each, 3 partners"
cando: "Can describe all aspects of present adult life"
hw: "Portfolio: write your \"My Life Today\" profile"

lessons.title: My career story — past simple review
grammar: Past simple: regular + irregular review + time expressions (in 2015, after that, then)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-11"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Past Simple '-ed' Endings","explain":"The suffix '-ed' can be pronounced in three different ways: /t/, /d/, or /ɪd/.","examples":[{"pattern":"worked","ipa":"/wɜːkt/","word":"worked"},{"pattern":"played","ipa":"/pleɪd/","word":"played"},{"pattern":"started","ipa":"/ˈstɑː.tɪd/","word":"started"}],"extension":"Use /ɪd/ only when the base verb ends in 't' or 'd'. For most others, it's just a quick /t/ or /d/ sound.","visual":"⌚"}]
speaking: "Tell your career story in 3 minutes to a partner"
listening: "A career biography"
reading: "A short professional biography"
writing: "6-sentence career history"
task: "Career timeline: draw and present your career path"
cando: "Can narrate a basic career history in past simple"
hw: "Write your career history as a timeline with sentences"

lessons.title: What were you doing? — past continuous
grammar: Past continuous: was/were + -ing; contrast with past simple (while, when)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-12"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Weak forms of 'was' and 'were'","explain":"In sentences, these auxiliary verbs are usually unstressed and sound weak.","examples":[{"pattern":"I was","ipa":"/aɪ wəz/","word":"I was working"},{"pattern":"They were","ipa":"/ðeɪ wə/","word":"They were talking"}],"extension":"Instead of saying 'WAZ' or 'WERE' strongly, use the schwa sound /ə/ to sound more natural.","visual":"☁️"}]
speaking: "Tell a \"when it happened\" story using both tenses"
listening: "An eyewitness account using both tenses"
reading: "A short news-style account"
writing: "3 sentences: \"I was ___ when ___\""
task: "Alibi game: where were you, what were you doing?"
cando: "Can use past continuous for an ongoing past action"
hw: "Write about what you were doing yesterday at specific times"

lessons.title: I used to… — past habits and states
grammar: Used to + infinitive for past habits and states no longer true
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-13"
pronunciation: [{"point":"The 'used to' Liaison","explain":"The 'd' in 'used' and the 't' in 'to' blend into a single /tu/ sound.","examples":[{"pattern":"used to","ipa":"/ˈjuːs.tə/","word":"I used to live..."}],"tip":"It sounds like 'yoos-ta'.","visual":"🔗"}]
speaking: "Tell a partner 3 things you used to do but don't anymore"
listening: "People comparing past and present habits"
reading: "A \"My life before and after\" article"
writing: "5 \"used to\" sentences comparing past and present life"
task: "Before and after: draw a timeline and present"
cando: "Can describe past habits using \"used to\""
hw: "Write a paragraph: \"My life before and now\""

lessons.title: Have you ever…? — life experiences
grammar: Present perfect: have/has + past participle + ever/never for experience
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-14"
pronunciation: [{"point":"Been vs Gone","explain":"Differentiate between these two participles in connected speech.","examples":[{"pattern":"been","ipa":"/biːn/","word":"I've been to Paris"},{"pattern":"gone","ipa":"/ɡɒn/","word":"He's gone to Paris"}],"extension":"'Been' implies a return, while 'Gone' implies the person is still there. Pronunciation is clear: /iː/ vs /ɒ/.","visual":"🛫↔️🛬"}]
speaking: "Ask a partner 5 \"Have you ever…?\" questions"
listening: "An interview about life experiences"
reading: "A \"bucket list\" article"
writing: "5 sentences: \"I have / have never ___\""
task: "\"Have you ever…?\" class survey — find someone who has"
cando: "Can ask and talk about life experiences using present perfect"
hw: "Write a \"Things I have and haven't done\" list with sentences"

lessons.title: I have just… — recent events
grammar: Present perfect: just, already, yet — meaning and position
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-15"
speaking: "Give 3 \"just/already\" updates to a partner"
listening: "A series of short updates (\"I've just heard…\")"
reading: "A news update text with just/already/yet"
writing: "5 sentences using just, already and yet"
task: "Office updates game: report news using just/already/yet"
cando: "Can report recent events using just, already and yet"
hw: "Write a \"news update\" paragraph about your week"

lessons.title: Since when? For how long? — duration
grammar: Present perfect: for + period, since + point in time; How long have you…?
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-16"
speaking: "Ask 3 partners: \"How long have you worked here/lived there?\""
listening: "People discussing how long they have lived/worked somewhere"
reading: "A \"How long have you been here?\" magazine feature"
writing: "5 sentences about duration using for and since"
task: "Duration timeline: display your for/since facts to the class"
cando: "Can ask and say how long something has been the case"
hw: "Write a paragraph using for and since about your life"

lessons.title: Telling a story — narrative skills
grammar: Past simple + past continuous + present perfect (mixed past narrative)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-17"
speaking: "Tell a 2-minute personal story to a partner"
listening: "A personal narrative story (3 minutes)"
reading: "A short story with mixed past tenses"
writing: "120-word story about a memorable event"
task: "Story competition: most interesting true story"
cando: "Can tell a coherent personal story in the past"
hw: "Write a 150-word personal narrative"

lessons.title: A difficult situation — problem narration
grammar: Past tenses + wish/if only (intro): "I wish I had…"
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-18"
speaking: "Describe a difficult situation you faced (2 minutes)"
listening: "Someone describing a difficult work situation"
reading: "A problem-solution narrative"
writing: "100-word narrative about a challenge"
task: "Problem sharing: listen and give advice using \"you should have\""
cando: "Can narrate a difficult past situation"
hw: "Write about a difficult situation and how you handled it"

lessons.title: Reporting what people said
grammar: Reported speech: say/tell + tense shift (present → past)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-19"
speaking: "Report what a partner told you to the class"
listening: "Reported speech examples"
reading: "A short article with reported quotes"
writing: "5 reported speech sentences"
task: "Telephone message relay: report what you heard"
cando: "Can report what someone said using basic reported speech"
hw: "Write 5 sentences reporting a recent conversation"

lessons.title: Unit 2 Review — My past
grammar: Past simple, past continuous, used to, present perfect (all uses)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-20"
speaking: "Oral: tell your \"life story\" so far (3 minutes)"
listening: "Listening test: mixed past tenses (15 items)"
reading: "A full narrative text"
writing: "200-word biographical paragraph"
task: "Life story presentation to a small group"
cando: "Can narrate personal history using all past forms"
hw: "Portfolio: write your best personal narrative"

lessons.title: I'm going to change — plans and intentions
grammar: Going to + infinitive for intention; present continuous for fixed arrangements
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-21"
speaking: "Tell a partner your top 3 plans for the next 6 months"
listening: "People discussing future plans"
reading: "A \"My goals for next year\" article"
writing: "\"My plans\" paragraph (5–6 sentences)"
task: "Goal-setting workshop: share and compare plans"
cando: "Can describe future plans and intentions using going to"
hw: "Write your top 5 goals for the next year"

lessons.title: It will be… — predictions and decisions
grammar: Will: predictions and spontaneous decisions; contrast with going to
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-22"
speaking: "Make 3 predictions about your life in 5 years"
listening: "Someone making predictions about their future"
reading: "A short prediction article"
writing: "5 will sentences (predictions + spontaneous decisions)"
task: "Future prediction game: class makes predictions"
cando: "Can use will for predictions and spontaneous decisions"
hw: "Write a paragraph: \"My life in 10 years\""

lessons.title: What are you doing on Friday? — arrangements
grammar: Present continuous for fixed future arrangements
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-23"
speaking: "Discuss what you are doing next week with a partner"
listening: "A diary being discussed"
reading: "A diary for next week"
writing: "Your schedule for next week in full sentences"
task: "Diary clash: find a time when everyone is free"
cando: "Can talk about fixed future arrangements"
hw: "Write an email confirming a future appointment"

lessons.title: If you work hard… — real conditions
grammar: First conditional: if + present simple + will
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-24"
speaking: "Give 3 pieces of advice using first conditionals"
listening: "Advice using first conditionals"
reading: "A \"success tips\" article with conditionals"
writing: "5 first conditional sentences"
task: "Conditional advice clinic: give real advice"
cando: "Can use first conditional for real situations"
hw: "Write 5 first conditional sentences about career decisions"

lessons.title: What would you do? — imagined situations
grammar: Second conditional (intro): if + past + would; "I would love to / If I had…"
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-25"
speaking: "Discuss: \"What would you do if you won €100,000?\""
listening: "Someone describing their dream job"
reading: "A \"What would you do if…?\" magazine feature"
writing: "3 second conditional sentences about dreams"
task: "Dream job pitch: if I could do any job, I would…"
cando: "Can use second conditional for imagined situations"
hw: "Write a paragraph about your ideal life"

lessons.title: Making decisions — comparing options
grammar: Comparatives and superlatives (extended); "It is better to… / the best option is…"
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-26"
speaking: "Compare two career paths or cities using comparatives"
listening: "People comparing two options"
reading: "A pros/cons article"
writing: "A pros and cons list as full sentences"
task: "Decision debate: buy or rent? Remote or office?"
cando: "Can compare options and justify a decision"
hw: "Write a 100-word pros and cons analysis"

lessons.title: What should I do? — giving advice
grammar: Should/shouldn't; "Why don't you + infinitive"; imperative for strong advice
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-27"
speaking: "Role-play: give advice to someone about a problem"
listening: "A radio advice programme"
reading: "A problem page with advice column"
writing: "A reply to a problem page letter"
task: "Advice circle: each person presents a problem, others advise"
cando: "Can ask for and give advice in real adult contexts"
hw: "Write a letter of advice to a friend with a problem"

lessons.title: I need to… — obligation and necessity
grammar: Need to / must / have to — necessity; mustn't for prohibition
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-28"
speaking: "Discuss: what do you need to do this week?"
listening: "Work instructions using obligation verbs"
reading: "A workplace rules document"
writing: "A list of 5 things you must do this week"
task: "Work priorities task: rank what must be done today"
cando: "Can express obligation and necessity using modal verbs"
hw: "Write a to-do list with modal verbs for each item"

lessons.title: Making requests and offers
grammar: Could you / Would you mind -ing / Shall I… / Would you like me to…
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-29"
speaking: "Practise 3 polite requests with a partner"
listening: "Formal and informal request dialogues"
reading: "A series of emails making requests"
writing: "A formal email with 2 polite requests"
task: "Office role-play: chain of requests at work"
cando: "Can make and respond to polite requests"
hw: "Write a formal email making 3 polite requests"

lessons.title: Unit 3 Review — My future
grammar: Going to, will, present continuous (future), first + second conditional, should, must
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-30"
speaking: "Oral: describe future plans and give someone advice (3 min)"
listening: "Listening test: future language (15 items)"
reading: "A \"My plans for the future\" article"
writing: "\"My future plans\" essay (200 words)"
task: "Future planning simulation: life at 50"
cando: "Can discuss the future using all A1 future structures"
hw: "Portfolio: write \"My plans for the next 5 years\""

lessons.title: Describing people — personality in depth
grammar: Adjective + preposition: good at, bad at, interested in, worried about
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-31"
speaking: "Describe 3 people you know using 3 adjectives each"
listening: "Descriptions of colleagues"
reading: "A character sketch"
writing: "100-word character description"
task: "Ideal colleague: build a profile together"
cando: "Can describe personality with adjectives and prepositions"
hw: "Write a description of your ideal manager"

lessons.title: The person who… — relative clauses
grammar: Defining relative clauses: who (people), which/that (things), where (places)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-32"
speaking: "Define 5 words using \"it is something that/which…\""
listening: "Relative clauses in descriptions"
reading: "A text rich in relative clauses"
writing: "5 sentences with relative clauses"
task: "Definition game: describe a word using a relative clause"
cando: "Can use defining relative clauses to describe people and things"
hw: "Write 5 descriptions using relative clauses"

lessons.title: Agreeing and discussing — question tags
grammar: Question tags: positive statement → negative tag; negative → positive
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-33"
speaking: "Practise agreeing and using tags with a partner"
listening: "Natural conversation with question tags"
reading: "A dialogue with agreement markers"
writing: "5 sentences with appropriate question tags"
task: "Discussion: agree or disagree with 5 statements"
cando: "Can use question tags in natural conversation"
hw: "Write a dialogue that includes 4 question tags"

lessons.title: Could you tell me…? — indirect questions
grammar: Indirect questions: statement word order after question intro
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-34"
speaking: "Practise 4 indirect questions in a role-play"
listening: "Polite professional enquiries"
reading: "A formal email with indirect questions"
writing: "4 indirect question forms"
task: "Information request at work or a service"
cando: "Can use indirect questions for polite professional communication"
hw: "Write an email using 3 indirect questions"

lessons.title: What do you think? — opinions and discussions
grammar: Opinion phrases: "I think/believe that + clause"; agreeing/disagreeing
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-35"
speaking: "Give your opinion on 3 topics to a partner"
listening: "A short discussion with opinions"
reading: "An opinion piece"
writing: "100-word opinion paragraph"
task: "Class opinion poll: vote and justify"
cando: "Can express and justify an opinion"
hw: "Write a 100-word opinion on a topic of your choice"

lessons.title: Talking about culture — customs and norms
grammar: Present simple for general truths; "It is + adjective + to + infinitive"
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-36"
speaking: "Compare cultural norms at work with a partner"
listening: "People comparing cultural norms"
reading: "A short cultural comparison text"
writing: "5 sentences about cultural norms in your country"
task: "Cultural differences discussion: is it polite to…?"
cando: "Can discuss cultural norms and compare customs"
hw: "Write a short guide to your country's work culture"

lessons.title: A community event — social English
grammar: Inviting: "Would you like to…?" / "Could you come to…?" Accepting/declining politely
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-37"
speaking: "Role-play: invite and respond to 3 different events"
listening: "Social invitation conversations"
reading: "An invitation and two replies (accept + decline)"
writing: "A formal invitation and an informal one"
task: "Event planning simulation: organise a work event"
cando: "Can invite, accept and decline invitations"
hw: "Write a formal invitation and a polite decline"

lessons.title: On the phone — telephone English
grammar: Formal telephone phrases; modal verbs for politeness (could, would)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-38"
speaking: "Role-play: 3 different phone situations"
listening: "3 telephone conversations"
reading: "Phone message scripts"
writing: "A phone message from dictation"
task: "Phone relay: message passed through 3 people"
cando: "Can make and receive professional phone calls"
hw: "Write a script for a professional phone call"

lessons.title: Writing an email — formal and informal
grammar: Formal vs informal register; tenses in emails (present + past + future)
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-39"
speaking: "Discuss: what makes an email professional?"
listening: "An email being read aloud"
reading: "Formal vs informal emails — compare"
writing: "A formal and informal version of the same email"
task: "Email correction challenge: fix 6 register errors"
cando: "Can write a simple formal and informal email"
hw: "Write a formal email and an informal one on the same topic"

lessons.title: Unit 4 Review — Relationships and communication
grammar: Relative clauses, question tags, indirect questions, opinion phrases, modal verbs
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-40"
speaking: "Role-play simulation: 3 workplace communication scenarios"
listening: "Extended listening: social and professional dialogues"
reading: "A full social/professional text"
writing: "150-word professional email"
task: "Communication challenge: all Unit 4 skills in one task"
cando: "Can communicate naturally in social and professional adult contexts"
hw: "Portfolio: best email + description"

lessons.title: My opinions on the world — discussion topics
grammar: Mixed A1 grammar in discussion; because + connectors: however, so, but
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-41"
speaking: "Discuss 2 topics using opinion language"
listening: "A short radio discussion (3 speakers)"
reading: "A short opinion article"
writing: "100-word opinion paragraph on a topic"
task: "Class debate on a simple real-world topic"
cando: "Can discuss real-world topics at A1 with simple opinions"
hw: "Write a 120-word opinion on a topic you care about"

lessons.title: Real conversations — fluency practice
grammar: Review of all A1 grammar through fluency tasks
vocabulary: ALL A1 verbs in natural speech
ALL A1 adjectives in natural speech
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-42"
speaking: "Extended conversation: 5 minutes on a topic"
listening: "Authentic short conversations (TV/radio extract)"
reading: "A full dialogue — analyse and discuss"
writing: "Self-record a 2-minute monologue"
task: "Partner feedback: fluency, grammar, vocabulary"
cando: "Can maintain a short extended conversation at A1"
hw: "Write a script and then speak without reading it"

lessons.title: Building vocabulary — word families and collocations
grammar: Collocations: do + noun, make + noun, have + noun, take + noun
vocabulary: make a decision / progress / a mistake
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-43"
speaking: "Collocation matching game"
listening: "Collocations in authentic short clips"
reading: "A text — find and note collocations"
writing: "Write 8 collocations in full sentences"
task: "Collocation bingo"
cando: "Can use common collocations naturally"
hw: "Create a collocation word map for 5 key verbs"

lessons.title: Pronunciation — stress and intonation
grammar: Word and sentence stress; rising vs falling intonation in questions
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-44"
speaking: "Drill: say sentences with correct stress pattern"
listening: "Listen and mark stress; identify question intonation"
reading: "Read sentences aloud with marked stress"
writing: "Write stress marks on 10 words"
task: "Pronunciation challenge: read and record"
cando: "Can produce correct word stress in known vocabulary"
hw: "Learn stress patterns for 20 key A1 words"

lessons.title: Reading strategies — understanding more
grammar: Mixed A1 grammar in reading context
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-45"
speaking: "Discuss: main idea vs detail in a text"
listening: "How to read strategically"
reading: "A text using skimming then scanning"
writing: "A summary of a 200-word text"
task: "Speed reading: who finds the answer first?"
cando: "Can use skimming and scanning to read efficiently"
hw: "Read one article at home and write a 50-word summary"

lessons.title: Writing paragraphs — structure and cohesion
grammar: Discourse connectors: firstly, also, however, for example, in conclusion
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-46"
speaking: "Discuss: what makes a good paragraph?"
listening: "A short structured talk"
reading: "3 model paragraphs — identify structure"
writing: "A structured paragraph on a given topic"
task: "Peer editing: improve a partner's paragraph"
cando: "Can write a structured paragraph with connectors"
hw: "Write a paragraph on your favourite topic using full structure"

lessons.title: A2 preview — What's coming next?
grammar: Past simple vs present perfect (contrast); going to vs will (contrast)
vocabulary: A2 preview vocabulary: conditionals, reported speech, comparisons
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-47"
speaking: "Discuss: what do you find hardest in English?"
listening: "A short A2-level dialogue"
reading: "A short A2-level article"
writing: "Your personal language learning goals"
task: "A1 reflection: what have you learned?"
cando: "Can identify areas to develop at A2"
hw: "Write your 5 goals for A2"

lessons.title: Revision day — grammar
grammar: ALL A1 grammar structures — error-focused revision
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-48"
speaking: "Grammar auction game"
listening: "Dictation: 15 A1 sentences"
reading: "Grammar reading: identify and correct errors"
writing: "Grammar test: all A1 structures"
task: "Error correction relay"
cando: "Can recognise and correct common A1 grammar errors"
hw: "Complete a personal grammar correction log"

lessons.title: Oral assessment preparation
grammar: ALL A1 grammar in spoken context
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-49"
speaking: "Mock oral exam: 5 minutes (introduction + topic)"
listening: "Practice listening tasks"
reading: "Oral exam preparation texts"
writing: "Preparation notes"
task: "Peer assessment using scoring rubric"
cando: "Can complete a full A1 oral assessment task"
hw: "Prepare and practise your oral exam speech"

lessons.title: Final assessment and reflection
grammar: ALL A1 grammar
lessons.teacher_notes:
code: "A1-50"
speaking: "A1 oral assessment: 5 minutes"
listening: "A1 listening test (20 items)"
reading: "A1 reading test (full text + questions)"
writing: "A1 writing test: email + paragraph"
task: "Portfolio review"
cando: "Can demonstrate all A1 competencies"
hw: "Write a reflection: \"What I can do now in English\""
